sections.paragraph:
fut. יִרְחַק inf. רָחֳקָה-

(1) to go away far, to recede from any one; followed by מִן Ecc 3:5 Job 30:10 (Chald., Syr., Eth., id. The primary sense appears to have been transitive; to thrust away, to repel, i.q. דָּחַק).

(2) to be afar off, to be distant, remote, Psa 103:12 followed by מִן and מֵעַל Jer 2:5 Eze 8:6 11:15 44:10 . Often used of God, as being far off from granting help, i.e. as refusing aid, Psa 22:12, 20 Psa 22:20 35:22 of men who abhor fraud, Exo 23:7 Isa 54:14 and on the other hand, from the law of God, Psa 119:150 they are far off from safety, Job 5:4.

Niphal, to be removed, Ecc 12:6 כתיב.

Piel, רִחַק to move far off, to remove, Isa 6:12 metaph. Isa 29:13.

Hiphil-

(1) trans. i.q. Piel, Job 13:21 19:13 Psa 103:12, “he (God) hath removed our transgressions from us,” i.e. he forgives them to us. Followed by an inf. ( Psa 55:8) and a gerund, adv. חִרְחִיק לָלֶכֶת to go far off, Exo 8:24. Inf. הַרְחֵק and adv., far off, Gen 21:16. Hence-

(2) intrans. to go away far, Gen 44:4 Jos 8:4.

Derivatives, רָחֹוק, מֶרְחָק, and-
